# SEO Notes — Shibam Coffee Atlanta

How structured data, canonical URLs, and the sitemap are implemented
across this site, and how to keep them correct as content changes.

## 1. Structured data (JSON-LD)

Every page hardcodes a `CafeOrCoffeeShop` JSON-LD block in `<head>`. It is
**static HTML, not generated by JavaScript** — this is deliberate, so
crawlers that don't execute JS (and Google's structured-data testing
tools) always see complete, identical business data regardless of page.

The block is byte-for-byte identical across all 8 HTML files except for
the `image` value, which can differ per page if you set page-specific
hero images. Fields used:

- `name`, `parentOrganization` — brand identity (Shibam Coffee Atlanta /
  Shibam Coffee Co.).
- `address` — full `PostalAddress`, must match the visible NAP text on
  every page exactly (see section "NAP consistency" below).
- `telephone` — E.164 format `+14703596586`.
- `openingHoursSpecification` — two entries: Sun–Thu 08:00–22:00 and
  Fri–Sat 08:00–00:00, in 24-hour `HH:MM` format per schema.org spec.
- `servesCuisine`, `priceRange` — categorical info Google uses for
  Knowledge Panel / local pack display.
- `hasMap` — a plain Google Maps search URL (not the embed iframe URL).
- `geo.latitude` / `geo.longitude` — currently placeholder values
  (`34.0432` / `-84.2778`). **Replace with the exact coordinates from
  the Google Business Profile listing before launch** — see the README
  "Before you go live" checklist.
- `sameAs` — social profile URLs, used for entity disambiguation.
- `url`, `image` — site URL and a representative photo; both use the
  `[SITE_URL]` placeholder convention (see section 3).

`index.html` additionally carries a `WebSite` JSON-LD block (with
`name`, `url`, and a `SearchAction` potentialAction) — this schema type
only belongs on the homepage, not on subpages, per Google's guidance on
sitelinks search box eligibility.

**To validate:** paste any page's source into
https://search.google.com/test/rich-results or
https://validator.schema.org/ after deploying.

### NAP consistency

"NAP" (Name, Address, Phone) must be character-for-character identical
everywhere it appears, because Google Business Profile matches your
website's NAP against the listing to build trust signals. NAP appears in
five places per page: visible header/footer text, the `nap-block` on
location.html, and the JSON-LD `address`/`telephone` fields — across all
6 primary pages. See the README section "How to update business hours"
for the grep commands to audit hours specifically; the same
search-and-verify approach applies to the street address and phone
number if either ever changes.

## 2. Canonical URLs

Every page has a self-referencing canonical tag:

```html
<link rel="canonical" href="[SITE_URL]/page-name.html">
```

The homepage canonical is `[SITE_URL]/` (root, no filename) — internal
links to the homepage also use `/` rather than `/index.html` for the
same reason, so there's only ever one valid URL per page from both a
crawler and an internal-linking perspective. `[SITE_URL]` is a literal
placeholder string (see section 3) so canonical tags are never wrong by
omission before launch — they simply won't resolve until the find/replace
is done, instead of silently pointing at the wrong host.

Open Graph (`og:url`, `og:image`) and Twitter Card (`twitter:image`) tags
follow the same `[SITE_URL]`-prefixed pattern, so social share previews
and canonical tags always agree on the page's "real" URL.

## 3. The `[SITE_URL]` placeholder

Every absolute URL reference that depends on the live domain — canonical
tags, OG/Twitter `url`/`image`, JSON-LD `url`/`image`/`hasMap`,
`robots.txt`'s `Sitemap:` line, and every `<loc>` in `sitemap.xml` — uses
the literal bracketed string `[SITE_URL]` instead of a guessed domain.
Once the Cloudflare Pages custom domain is live, replace it everywhere
in one pass:

```bash
grep -rl '\[SITE_URL\]' . | xargs sed -i 's|\[SITE_URL\]|https://www.shibamatlanta.com|g'
```

Run `grep -rn '\[SITE_URL\]' .` afterward to confirm zero matches remain
before considering the site launch-ready.

## 4. Sitemap

`sitemap.xml` lists the 6 primary pages (home, menu, catering-events,
about, location, contact) — legal pages (`privacy-policy.html`,
`terms.html`) are intentionally excluded since they're marked
`noindex` (see section 5) and have no SEO value to surface in search
results.

Each `<url>` entry has:
- `<loc>` — the page URL, using `[SITE_URL]`.
- `<lastmod>` — date of last meaningful content change, `YYYY-MM-DD`.
- `<changefreq>` — `weekly` for the homepage, `monthly` for the rest.
- `<priority>` — `1.0` for the homepage, `0.8` for the other 5 pages.

**When you add, remove, or rename a page:**
1. Add/update its `<url>` block in `sitemap.xml`.
2. Update its `<lastmod>`.
3. Confirm `robots.txt`'s `Sitemap:` line still points at
   `[SITE_URL]/sitemap.xml`.
4. Resubmit the sitemap in Google Search Console (Sitemaps → enter
   `sitemap.xml` → Submit) so Google re-crawls promptly instead of
   waiting for its next scheduled crawl.

## 5. robots.txt and noindex pages

`robots.txt` allows crawling of everything (`Allow: /`) and points to the
sitemap. The two legal pages carry a page-level
`<meta name="robots" content="noindex, follow">` instead of a
`robots.txt` disallow rule — this lets Google still follow links *out of*
those pages (e.g. back to the homepage) while keeping the pages
themselves out of search results, since they have no unique search
intent behind them.

## 6. Per-page SEO targets

Each page's `<title>` (under 60 characters) and `<meta name="description">`
(under 160 characters) target a distinct primary keyword so pages don't
compete with each other in search results:

| Page | Primary target |
|---|---|
| index.html | "Shibam Coffee Atlanta", "Yemeni coffee Alpharetta" |
| menu.html | "Shibam Coffee menu", specific drink names (Pistachio Latte, Adeni Chai) |
| catering-events.html | "coffee catering Alpharetta", "event catering North Fulton" |
| about.html | "Yemeni coffee shop Atlanta", brand/heritage story queries |
| location.html | "coffee shop near North Point Alpharetta", "coffee shop Alpharetta GA" |
| contact.html | "contact Shibam Coffee", "Shibam Coffee phone number" |

Every page has exactly one `<h1>`, descriptive `alt` text on every
content image (decorative/background images are marked
`aria-hidden="true"` instead), and `aria-label`s on icon-only links
(social icons, nav toggle) so screen readers announce their purpose.
